description: Add E2E tests covering the full user journey of discovering, installing, and uninstalling npm plugins through the side panel search box. Tests use NPM_CONFIG_PREFIX to isolate npm global installs to a temp directory, hitting the real npm registry. This fills the critical gap where no happy-path install/uninstall tests exist and no side panel UI tests exercise the search → install → uninstall flow.

userStories.title: API-level happy-path: install npm plugin, verify in registry, uninstall
userStories.description: Add E2E tests to plugin-management.e2e.ts that exercise the full plugin.install and plugin.remove happy paths at the WebSocket JSON-RPC level, using NPM_CONFIG_PREFIX to isolate global installs to a temp directory.
acceptanceCriteria: Test installs opentabs-plugin-slack via plugin.install with NPM_CONFIG_PREFIX pointing to a temp dir
Response has { ok: true, plugin: { name, displayName, version, toolCount } }
Plugin appears in /health endpoint with source 'npm'
Plugin tools appear in MCP tools/list
Test then removes the plugin via plugin.remove
Response has { ok: true }
Plugin no longer appears in /health or MCP tools/list
Temp prefix directory is cleaned up in the finally block

userStories.title: Side panel UI: search for plugins, see results under Available section
userStories.description: Add an E2E test that opens the side panel, types in the search bar, and verifies npm search results appear under the 'Available' section header as NpmPluginCard components.
acceptanceCriteria: Test types 'opentabs' in the search input
Test waits for the Available section header to appear
Test verifies at least one NpmPluginCard is visible with an Install button
Test verifies the search loading spinner appears during search
Test verifies clearing the search bar removes the Available section
userStories.notes:
Files:
- e2e/side-panel-npm-search.e2e.ts (new file)

Context:
The side panel search bar is in App.tsx (lines 360-386). When the user types, `handleSearchChange` debounces and calls `searchPlugins(query)` from bridge.ts, which sends `bg:searchPlugins` → background → WebSocket → server `plugin.search` → npm registry.

SearchResults.tsx renders the results:
- Line 96: `<SectionHeader>Installed</SectionHeader>` — section header for installed matches
- Line 135: `<SectionHeader>Available</SectionHeader>` — section header for npm results
- Lines 126-129: loading spinner while `npmSearching` is true
- Lines 137-145: `NpmPluginCard` for each available result
- Line 152: 'No results' when nothing matches

NpmPluginCard.tsx renders each result:
- Line 25: display name as `<span>` or `<a>` (after the npm link PR lands)
- Line 29: `<Badge>` with version
- Line 31: author text
- Line 33: Install `<Button>`

The search input in App.tsx:
```tsx
<input
  type="text"
  placeholder="Search plugins and tools..."
  value={searchQuery}
  onChange={e => handleSearchChange(e.target.value)}
  className="w-full rounded border-2 border-border bg-card px-3 py-1.5 pr-8 font-sans text-foreground text-sm placeholder:text-muted-foreground focus:border-primary focus:outline-none"
/>
```

Changes:
Create `e2e/side-panel-npm-search.e2e.ts` with a test that:

1. Standard setup: MCP server (hot=false) + extension context. The server needs NO special env — the default `OPENTABS_SKIP_NPM_DISCOVERY=1` is fine since we're testing the search UI, not auto-discovery. The search always hits the real npm registry regardless of SKIP_NPM_DISCOVERY.

2. Open side panel, wait for connected state.

3. Type 'opentabs' in the search input (`sidePanelPage.getByPlaceholder('Search plugins and tools...')`).

4. Wait for the 'Available' section header: `await expect(sidePanelPage.getByText('Available')).toBeVisible({ timeout: 15_000 })`. This confirms the search completed and results are rendered.

5. Verify at least one Install button is visible: `await expect(sidePanelPage.getByRole('button', { name: 'Install' }).first()).toBeVisible()`.

6. Clear the search: `await sidePanelPage.getByPlaceholder('Search plugins and tools...').fill('')`.

7. Verify 'Available' section disappears: `await expect(sidePanelPage.getByText('Available')).toBeHidden({ timeout: 5_000 })`.

Mark the test `test.slow()` since it hits the real npm registry.

Import from fixtures: `cleanupTestConfigDir`, `E2E_TEST_PLUGIN_DIR`, `expect`, `launchExtensionContext`, `startMcpServer`, `test`, `writeTestConfig`, `getPluginVersion`.
Import from helpers: `openSidePanel`, `setupAdapterSymlink`, `waitForExtensionConnected`, `waitForLog`.

Constraints:
- Do NOT modify any production code.
- Use `test.slow()` for the network-dependent search.
- The search debounce is 300ms — after typing, wait for results (the 'Available' header visibility assertion handles this).
- Use the standard E2E setup and cleanup pattern (try/finally with context.close, server.kill).

userStories.title: Side panel UI: install plugin via search results, verify it moves to Installed section
userStories.description: Add an E2E test that searches for a plugin, clicks the Install button on an NpmPluginCard, waits for the install to complete, and verifies the plugin moves from the Available section to the Installed section (appearing as a PluginCard). Uses NPM_CONFIG_PREFIX for isolation.
acceptanceCriteria: Test types a search query, waits for Available results
Test clicks the Install button on a specific NpmPluginCard
Test verifies the Install button shows a loading spinner during install
After install, the plugin appears in the Installed section as a PluginCard (accordion item)
The plugin no longer appears in the Available section (filtered out by installedShortNames)
The plugin card shows the correct display name

userStories.title: Side panel UI: uninstall plugin via PluginMenu, verify it disappears
userStories.description: Add an E2E test that uninstalls a previously installed npm plugin through the PluginMenu three-dot menu, confirms the removal dialog, and verifies the plugin disappears from the Installed list. This test continues from the install test's state (or installs first as setup).
acceptanceCriteria: Test has a plugin installed (either chained from US-003 or installs as setup)
Test expands the plugin card to reveal the three-dot menu trigger
Test clicks the three-dot menu and selects 'Uninstall'
Test confirms the removal in the confirmation dialog by clicking the 'Uninstall' button
After removal, the plugin card disappears from the side panel
The MCP tools/list no longer includes the plugin's tools
